title: Funding to Support the National Framework on Cancers Linked to Firefighting: Climate, nature and economy lens public statement - Canada.ca
content:
## Funding to Support the National Framework on Cancers Linked to Firefighting: Climate, nature and economy lens public statement
- Strategic Environmental Analysis
- Strategic Economic Analysis
- Cross-Cutting Considerations
In line with the Cabinet Directive on Strategic Environmental and Economic Assessment (SEEA), Health Canada conducted a strategic environmental analysis on the proposal for federal actions to support the National Framework on Cancers Linked to Firefighting (National Framework).
Through their work, firefighters are subject to high occupational exposures to certain chemicals and have greater incidence and risk of mortality from cancer relative to the general population. Health Canada will undertake activities to raise awareness of cancers linked to firefighting and promote information sharing, generate new scientific knowledge, develop clinical guidance, and address training needs, with the ultimate goal of improving access for firefighters to cancer prevention and treatment.
The proposal also includes funding for Statistics Canada to lead the development of a National Firefighter Cancer Registry.

## Strategic Environmental Analysis
Health Canada's actions are expected to generate greater awareness of cancers linked to firefighting and improve firefighters' access to cancer prevention and treatment. This work will also help fill research gaps for sub-populations of firefighters such as wildland firefighters, and address knowledge gaps on diagnostic testing for firefighters. While these actions will not have a direct impact on nature or biodiversity, maintaining a healthy and well-protected workforce of firefighters is essential for controlling the spread of fires. As such, this initiative may indirectly contribute to the preservation of natural habitats and the protection of species.

## Strategic Economic Analysis
In accordance with guidance under the Cabinet Directive on Strategic Environmental and Economic Assessment , a strategic economic analysis was not conducted due to the scope of the proposal.

## Cross-Cutting Considerations

### Federal Sustainable Development Strategy
The proposal supports Goal 13 of the 2022-2026 Federal Sustainable Development Strategy by taking action on climate change and its impacts, in recognition of increasingly severe wildfire seasons.
Indigenous firefighters represent a high proportion of wildland and remote firefighters, including those on-reserve. In these settings, firefighters report routinely working in hazardous situations with limited training and without personal protective equipment, adequate fire-mitigation resources, or enforced occupational health and safety standards. Health Canada will establish a national advisory group with representation from Indigenous organizations to better consider Indigenous firefighters' health-related needs. The proposal also includes funding to develop Canadian standards (e.g., procedural and equipment-based) to better protect the health and safety of wildland firefighters.
Statistics Canada will establish the National Firefighter Cancer Registry to better understand the cancer risks faced by different types of firefighters, including Indigenous firefighters. This will enable governments and Indigenous organizations to bridge information gaps over time.
Health Canada led a comprehensive engagement process, including multiple workshops with stakeholders in the firefighting, research, and health care communities to inform the development of the National Framework. Heath Canada published a What We Heard Report in May 2024 to capture the considerations and experiences shared throughout the consultation process.
